definitions: A woman whose 1964 murder in New York City became a symbol of bystander apathy.
The phenomenon of bystander apathy, often associated with the murder of Kitty Genovese, where individuals are less likely to offer help to a victim when other people are present.
examples: The Kitty Genovese case sparked widespread discussion about urban indifference.
Psychologists often refer to the Kitty Genovese incident when discussing the bystander effect.
